# Transcrição: A_simple_trick_to_approach_strangers_easier!

- **Fonte:** A_simple_trick_to_approach_strangers_easier!
- **Duração:** 01:07
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:07]

I remember when I first started practicing magic, I wasn't very good yet, but I would go out on Friday nights into the city to perform magic with a few friends. Now, at this point, I wouldn't classify us as magicians because we weren't professional magicians by any regards. But I remember what we used to say and in saying this, it gave us confidence to actually walk up to people and perform. We'd walk up to people and we say, hey, we've got a gig this weekend and we're just doing a bit of practice for our gig coming up. We're professional magicians and we'd love to show you a piece of magic because we found that when we just walk up to people and say, hey, do you want to see magic? People go, no, I don't know what this could be. This could be something terrible. And at the end of performing this trick, because we primed it with, oh, we're just practicing for a gig this weekend. One person asked us, how much do you guys charge to come out and do a private function? I was like, what? They want to book us? You know, and it was amazing because he then did book me. And to me in my mind right now, that's such a clear example of fake it till you make it because I genuinely was faking it. And then it led to a booked gig. And then at that point, once I did the professional book to gig, I felt professional. It's this really weird mental game we play with ourselves. We need validation before we feel we're a certain thing.
